author: codex-jellyfin

patch.value: Refresh Jellyfin 10.11.11 API and agent interface

Research the authoritative Jellyfin 10.11.11 API and the configured local instance, harden schema discovery when the server-hosted document is unavailable, verify intuitive help and structured output contracts, and ship read-only production evidence without exposing private configuration.

Live API-key and username/password authentication are proven without media/library mutation; Schema research uses the exact server-version OpenAPI contract even when the local Swagger endpoint fails; All CLI help, output formats, schemas, setup flows, and read-only E2E checks pass; No credential or instance-derived private data is tracked by Git
Give humans and agents a reliable complete command surface for current Jellyfin servers.
Makes API discovery and programmatic workflows resilient to server Swagger failures while preserving privacy and read-only safety.
A release-ready CLI with current-version schema evidence, validated command mappings, and fully documented local setup.
The configured Jellyfin 10.11.11 instance returns HTTP 500 for its local OpenAPI endpoint, making current readiness and research commands fail despite valid auth.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Started with all-status tracker inventory, current GitHub inventory, setup wizard validation, and read-only live auth checks.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Private credentials remain only under ~/.jellyfin-cli at restrictive permissions; repository research artifacts must be sanitized and reproducible.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Jellyfin publishes exact stable OpenAPI documents at repo.jellyfin.org/files/openapi/stable/, including 10.11.11; remote fetches must never receive the local API key.

message: Track Jellyfin 10.11.11 API and agent-interface delivery

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Authoritative research confirms Jellyfin documents the server Swagger UI under /api-docs/swagger/index.html and publishes exact stable OpenAPI files through repo.jellyfin.org. Live 10.11.11 local Swagger JSON currently fails with HTTP 500, so implementation will prefer local and fall back to exact official version without credentials.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Current program state: live authentication, usage-statistics resilience, OpenAPI source fallback, format validation, global help deduplication, and compiled read-only acceptance are implemented. Honest blockers remain genuine four-dimensional 100% coverage and the complete strict release-gate matrix.

patch.value: Authoritative research confirms Jellyfin documents Swagger under /api-docs/swagger/index.html and publishes exact stable OpenAPI files through repo.jellyfin.org. The first local probe returned a transient HTTP 500; repeated validation now serves all 429 operations on 10.11.11. The implementation therefore prefers local OpenAPI and retains an exact-version official fallback without forwarding credentials.
